scenarios.description: children() returns empty Elements when no child nodes exist (childNodeSize()==0)
scenarios.precondition: Create Element e with no children; no mocks

scenarios.description: children() returns empty Elements when childNodeSize()>0 but no children are Element instances (only TextNode)
scenarios.precondition: Create Element e and append two TextNode children; no mocks

scenarios.description: children() returns single-element when exactly one Element child exists (one iteration yields match)
scenarios.precondition: Create Element parent and append one Element child; no mocks
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Elements contains exactly that child at index 0

scenarios.description: children() returns multiple Elements preserving insertion order for mixed-node list
scenarios.precondition: Create Element parent and append TextNode and two Element children interleaved; no mocks
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Elements contains the two Element children in original order
pseudocode: assert result.size() == 2; assert result.get(0) == li1; assert result.get(1) == li2;

scenarios.description: children() uses cachedChildren() on second call to avoid re-filtering
scenarios.precondition: Create Element parent and append one Element child, call children() once to populate cache; no mocks
scenarios.expectedResult: second call returns same Elements instance content without re-filtering (size==1)

scenarios.description: children() reflects removal of Element children between calls (cache invalidated when structure changes)
scenarios.precondition: Create Element parent, append one Element child, call children() to cache, then remove child via empty(); no mocks
scenarios.expectedResult: after removal, children() returns empty Elements (size==0)
